purpose: Predicting the transmission chain of a carry trade unwind through asset classes.
components: Stage 1: FX volatility spike (carry currency depreciation)
Stage 2: Rates market disruption (rate differentials compress)
Stage 4: Credit spread widening (final stage, severity-determining)
Credit cycle position as primary severity discriminant
current_assessment: Inherited from iter_0009 at 6.5/10 confidence. August 2024 episode validated stages 1-3 but credit stage was contained (spreads below danger thresholds). Current credit spreads (IG ~110bp, HY ~380bp) favor contained scenario but 2025-2027 maturity wall introduces time-varying risk. Maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation (2Y negative vs 30Y slightly positive during unwind) is a post-2022 phenomenon that could cause back-end duration hedges to fail.
limitations: Based on limited sample of full-cycle unwinds
August 2024 episode may not be representative of future unwinds
Credit stage severity depends on cycle position which is itself hard to assess
Post-2022 maturity-dependent correlation shift may be transient
No specification of timing between stages
